subject: Databases
title: Query Rewriting
description: A fast breakdown of Query Rewriting for builders.

hook.text: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE YEAR(created_at) = 2024 can't use an index. Rewrite it and it flies.

buildup.text: Functions on indexed columns prevent index use. WHERE created_at >= '2024-01-01' AND created_at < '2025-01-01' uses it.

discovery.text: Replace OR with UNION ALL when each branch can use a different index. The planner handles each separately.

twist.text: Correlated subqueries can often be rewritten as JOINs. A JOIN lets the planner optimize both sides together.

climax.text: Small rewrites, big gains. The same logic expressed differently can be 100x faster.

punchline.text: Rewrite queries to match what indexes expect. Same result, faster path.

quiz.question: Why does WHERE YEAR(created_at) = 2024 prevent index usage?
options: The function wraps the indexed column, preventing the planner from matching it to the index
YEAR is not a valid SQL function
Index scans don't work on date columns
